mission_brief.title: PM superpower recovery mission
mission_brief.mission_summary: ProductOS Reference Workspace should help Product manager using ProductOS with Codex, Claude, Windsurf, or Antigravity style agents solve PMs still need one repo-native way to carry mission, research, prioritization, delivery, and review context through the full lifecycle without rebuilding the decision chain by hand.
mission_brief.target_user: Product manager using ProductOS with Codex, Claude, Windsurf, or Antigravity style agents
mission_brief.customer_problem: PMs still need a single repo-native way to move from mission and evidence through prioritization, delivery, review, and bounded communication outputs without rebuilding the story or losing provenance by hand.
mission_brief.business_goal: Make ProductOS credible for the first impatient customer by proving a coherent lifecycle-enrichment spine with governed research, prioritization, delivery traceability, and bounded visual outputs.
success_metrics: time from intake to reviewable PRD
time from PRD to aligned docs and deck
time from aligned artifact set to publish-ready deck or corridor
constraints: Keep PM approval explicit for decision-driving scope and release movement.
Preserve observed versus inferred claims when evidence is incomplete.
Do not broaden autonomous PM claims past the current evidence-backed release boundary.

mission_router.routing_rationale: Run the full PM loop from a mission-first discover start while keeping PM approval explicit at every decision-driving boundary.
stop_conditions: Stop if any phase loses provenance, reviewability, or PM approval visibility.
Stop if downstream phases outrun the accepted mission package.
Stop if release-facing claims exceed the current evidence-backed boundary.

operating_norms: Treat the repository as the system of record.
Keep PM approval explicit for decision-driving scope, stakeholder-facing output, and release movement.
Preserve observed versus inferred claims when evidence is incomplete.
Prefer the smallest coherent slice that can be reviewed and validated end to end.

mission_brief.next_action: Keep the mission routed through the strategy spine and complete P0/P1/P2 lifecycle-enrichment reconciliation before broadening any release claim; treat visual surfaces as one aligned output lane inside that governed lifecycle.

primary_outcomes: Make ProductOS credible for the first impatient customer by proving a coherent lifecycle-enrichment spine with governed research, prioritization, delivery traceability, and bounded visual outputs.
primary_kpis: time from intake to reviewable PRD
time from PRD to aligned docs and deck
time from aligned artifact set to publish-ready deck or corridor

active_phase_packet.phase_goal: Prioritize customer pain clarity, segmentation, and hypothesis sharpness over scale optimization.
question_prompts: What is the highest-signal decision the PM needs from discovery?
Which evidence is observed versus inferred for PM superpower recovery mission?
How should zero_to_one defaults change the discovery packet?

task_queue.title: Discovery task 1
task_queue.summary: Frame the problem in customer language before solution framing expands.

task_queue.title: Discovery task 2
task_queue.summary: Capture observed evidence and keep every inferred leap explicit.

task_queue.title: Discovery task 3
task_queue.summary: Build the first PM-visible backlog from the mission, not from generic templates.

recommended_pm_actions.summary: Approve the bounded discovery packet only after the evidence trail and next decision stay legible.
recommended_pm_actions.assumption_note: Task content is maturity-aware scaffolding until workspace-specific evidence replaces the starter defaults.

mission_control.current_task_summary: The bounded baseline gate is clear, so the next disciplined slice should promote deck, map, and workflow corridor outputs from one governed CLI surface instead of broadening research or distribution claims.

cockpit_state.request_summary: Operate the cleared bounded baseline from the repo CLI and move into the visual operating system release.
cockpit_state.current_focus: Promote the visual operating system across deck, map, and workflow corridor outputs while keeping PM review, proof posture, and publish safety explicit.

recommended_next_step.action_summary: Review the cleared bounded-baseline portfolio and begin the visual operating system release.
recommended_next_step.rationale: The bounded baseline is complete, so the next bounded decision is promoting the visual operating system rather than broadening governed research or publication scope.

active_agents.current_task: Keep the repo CLI surface, portfolio review, and next actions visible.

orchestration_state.coordination_summary: The bounded baseline now clears the frozen eval suite and portfolio truthfulness gate. The hardening cycle is complete, and the next step is promoting the visual operating system across deck, map, and workflow corridor outputs.

mission_control.current_task_summary: The bounded baseline gate is clear, so the next disciplined slice should promote deck, map, and workflow corridor outputs from one governed CLI surface instead of broadening research or distribution claims.

route_plan.objective: Turn live inbox notes and transcripts into a decision-ready discovery-operations path that can become the discover standard for the next version.
route_plan.rationale: This route is now promoted as the first completed next-version superpower slice.
route_plan.next_action: Keep the discover outputs as the current standard and hand the next bounded slice to the weekly PM autopilot route.

route_plan.objective: Package current ProductOS truth into readable docs and a stakeholder-ready deck through one aligned path.
route_plan.rationale: Alignment is already one of the strongest current slices and should stay usable during the next-version build.
route_plan.next_action: Refresh the readable docs and deck from the promoted discover package whenever the mission package changes.

route_plan.objective: Collapse current queue and release state into one supervised weekly operator path.
route_plan.rationale: The weekly PM autopilot is the next high-leverage slice after the discover loop.
route_plan.next_action: Keep the supervised weekly operator bundle active as part of the promoted baseline.

route_plan.objective: Refresh the portfolio review and improvement loop after every major bounded slice.
route_plan.rationale: Sub-5 features should always become explicit improvement work, and the loop must stay subordinate to frozen eval and decision-memory evidence before claiming promotion.
route_plan.next_action: Use the cleared score-refresh loop to choose the next bounded expansion slice.

route_plan.objective: Expand ProductOS into a governed visual operating system that renders aligned PM artifacts as decks, maps, and workflow corridors before broader publication claims broaden.
route_plan.rationale: The bounded baseline is already evidence-backed, so the next disciplined slice should package that truth into reviewable visual outputs instead of broadening research or distribution scope.
route_plan.next_action: Promote the visual lane, keep deck and corridor publish checks explicit, and defer broader research and distribution work until the bounded release is proven.

queue_impacts.reason: The bounded baseline gate is clear, so the next review should promote the visual operating system instead of broadening governed research or publication scope.

handoff_log.message: The discover route transferred the current strategy and problem framing into one reusable research packet before downstream prioritization expands.

insights.reusable_recommendation: Reuse the mission intake, KPI framing, and approval posture before opening a parallel workspace flow.
